channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, a Proponent of Drone Warfare, Is Forced Out
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov announced he was leaving the ministry on Wednesday after conflicts with generals and military contractors over the role of innovative weaponry.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov in Kyiv last March.

item.title: Mass Protests Across Ukraine Oppose Ouster of Defense Minister
item.description: Demonstrators demanded the reinstatement of Mykhailo Fedorov, who had come to symbolize Ukraine’s success in using drones to strike back against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd, Andrew E. Kramer and Oleksandr Chubko
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Protesting the dismissal of Ukraine’s defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, in Kharkiv on Thursday.

item.title: What to Know About Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s Ousted Defense Minister
item.description: Mr. Fedorov became the youthful face of Ukraine’s successful drone warfare program. The move to replace him has prompted street protests.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd and Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov in Kyiv, Ukraine’s capital, on Thursday. His appointment was seen as an indication that President Volodymyr Zelensky saw drones as the best bet for defeating Russia.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Turns Skies Orange Across the U.S., and Democrats Make a Big Shift on Israel
item.description: Plus, why Gen Z is turning to trade schools.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart and Julia Moskin
item.media:description: People walking in downtown Toronto. Smoke from forest fires in Canada has caused poor air quality in many places in North America.

item.title: In Iran war, Trump Risks Another American ‘Forever War’
item.description: President Trump, who promised to “end wars,” not start them, may have fallen into a familiar presidential trap.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: A judiciary building damaged in the war as seen from the Golestan palace, in central Tehran, Iran, this month.

item.title: Diesel Prices Hit $5 a Gallon Again, Up 33% Since Start of Iran War
item.description: U.S. diesel prices first surpassed $5 a gallon in March. Renewed fighting in the Persian Gulf and reduced refinery capacity is once again pushing up prices.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A driver pumping gas at a gas station, in Austin, Texas, on Friday.

item.title: Maps Show Where Wildfire Smoke Will Darken Skies on Thursday
item.description: Here’s what to know about the smoke, and when it might end.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:description: Smoke gave the sky in Toronto an orange hue on Wednesday.

item.title: Dozens Are Rescued in Texas Flash Floods as Rain Intensifies
item.description: More than 75 people have been rescued, said Gov. Greg Abbott, who issued a disaster declaration in 59 counties.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones, Christine Hauser and Isabella Kwai

item.title: Trump Administration Argues for Fencing Off Park Next to White House
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts is set to consider the administration’s proposal regarding Lafayette Square Park, which has been the site of numerous protests.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-Palestinian demonstration at Lafayette Square Park in 2023. President Trump and the Secret Service have cited security concerns about such protests.

item.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs on Brazil
item.description: The United States accused the country of unfair trade practices. The tariff will replace ones that the Supreme Court struck down.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ana Ionova
item.media:description: President Trump first imposed tariffs on Brazil last year in an effort to help former President Jair Bolsonaro avoid a jail sentence.

item.title: Hegseth Plans to Screen All Troops, Including Women, for Low Testosterone
item.description: Pete Hegseth, as defense secretary, has sought to cultivate an image as a manosphere-friendly leader.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Azeen Ghorayshi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The goal, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a social media message, was a “High-T Department of War.”

item.title: The F.D.A. Approves a New Pill to Slash Cholesterol Levels
item.description: The new pills can lower cholesterol levels far below what can be achieved with statins, clinical trials have shown.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata
item.media:description: Merck’s cholesterol drug enlicitide, currently an injection prescribed by cardiologists, will be available as a daily pill prescribed by a primary care physician.

item.title: Almost Half of House Democrats Vote to End Aid to Israel
item.description: The measure failed, but the level of support among Democrats exposed a stark shift in the party away from backing the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries, the Democratic minority leader and a staunch supporter of Israel, hosted two caucus meetings to discuss the amendment, a highly unusual move that indicated how fraught the politics around Israel have become for Democrats.

item.title: Video Pushed Local Police to Come Clean. D.H.S. Is Ignoring the Lessons.
item.description: The agency has said little about two fatal encounters in the past week. Local police departments have learned to be forthcoming.
item.dc:creator: Shaila Dewan and Esmé E. Deprez
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo near the site where he was killed in Houston.

item.title: Members of Congress Intensify Criticism of Immigration Detention Center
item.description: Lawmakers are continuing to urge action at Delaney Hall in Newark, where the conditions have been decried by detainees and demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley
item.media:description: Demonstrations outside Delaney Hall in Newark have gone on for weeks.

item.title: Investigators Seek Information From Government Officials as Part of Air Force One Leak Investigation
item.description: After The New York Times reported on security concerns related to the Qatari-donated jet, the F.B.I. sought to speak with several people who flew aboard the plane with President Trump last week and asked for their phones.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Glenn Thrush
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump on July 1 at Joint Base Andrews next to the jet donated by Qatar, before its first flight as Air Force One.

item.title: Our Top Editor on Subpoenas of Times Journalists
item.description: The Trump administration issued subpoenas to reporters who wrote about security concerns involving the new Air Force One. Joe Kahn, the executive editor of The New York Times, responds to what he calls a “naked attempt to intimidate” the newspaper.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kahn, Jillian Eugenios, Megan DiTrolio, Santiago García Muñoz, Alejandro Soto Goico, Zach Caldwell, Luke Piotrowski and Mike Abrams
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: New York Times Files Motion to Quash Subpoenas of Its Journalists
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel grand jury testimony as part of an investigation into Times reporting on the president’s new Air Force One jet.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: President Trump boarding the new Air Force One at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland this month.

item.title: Senators Grill Justice Dept. Officials on New York Times Subpoenas
item.description: Democrats questioned the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, and a U.S. attorney about subpoenas issued to Times journalists who reported on security concerns with the president’s new plane.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times, Alex Kent/The New York Times

item.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
item.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Hari Kumar
item.media:credit: Priyadarshini Ravichandran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The port city of Visakhapatnam in the state of Andhra Pradesh is rapidly transforming into India’s coastal gateway for global tech infrastructure, fueled by massive A.I. data centers.

item.title: TSMC Adds $100 Billion to Its U.S. Spending Plan
item.description: The pledge from the Taiwanese chip-making giant brought the total commitment to its fast-growing operations in Arizona to $265 billion.
item.dc:creator: Steve Lohr
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times

item.title: Europe Finds It Hard to Break Up With American and Chinese Technology
item.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Mark Landler
item.media:description: Sébastien Lecornu, the French prime minister, has called for France to increase its independence from American technology firms, announcing plans to replace Zoom with a French-developed version.

item.title: The Army Veteran Killed Outside His ‘Trump House’
item.description: Kerry Sheron could often be found waving and nodding to those who passed his home, which stood out in Escondido, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photograph of Kerry Sheron and his wife, Maria Garcia, hanging on the fence outside their home.

item.title: The Hunt for the Counterfeiter Trying to Make the Perfect Bill
item.description: On the trail of the world’s best counterfeit artists.
item.dc:creator: Yudhijit Bhattacharjee

item.title: Meet Earth’s Newest Monkey. (And Check Out Its Orange Lips.)
item.description: It is only the fifth new African monkey identified as a new species in the past 75 years.
item.dc:creator: Kate Golembiewski

item.title: A Vision of a ‘Society Without Capitalism’
item.description: Can democratic socialism redeem the left without destroying capitalism?
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat and Sophia Alvarez Boyd

item.title: Teddy’s Place
item.description: A visit to the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Will Warasila for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library.

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Joe Biden’s New Memoir
item.description: “It’s the second book Biden has written,” Fallon said of the former president. “The first was ‘The Odyssey.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Sam Neill’s Cause of Death Was Pneumonia, Actor’s Agent Confirms
item.description: The beloved New Zealand actor best known for his turn in “Jurassic Park” had “fought and beaten” lymphoma before he fell ill, according to a statement.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:description: Sam Neill died on Monday from pneumonia, his agent confirmed on Thursday.
